Repeat against a cron-triggered run via the triggers HTTP surface and assert extensions.scheduledFor === null AND lastScheduledRun === runId AND lastRunId === runId AND trigger:'cron'. Idempotent + self-cleaning per scripts/acceptance conventions.

steps.title: Flip R-01 status in ROADMAP.md + record mulch convention
steps.description: Edit ROADMAP.md:47 from `[partially shipped]` to `[shipped — producer side; consumer-side reads ride with R-04]`. Update the cross-repo dashboard row (line 1501) if needed; update sequencing entry at line 1539 to reflect closure. `ml record runs convention --description 'spawnRun writes warren-namespaced seed extensions {role,trigger,lastRunId,lastRunAt} via src/seeds-cli/updateExtensions; failures degrade to system events, never abort the run' --evidence-seeds warren-5a9c`. Then `ml sync && sd sync && git push`.

risks: sd not on PATH inside burrow workers: warren already shells out via SpawnFn elsewhere, but acceptance must verify the host-side path used by warren-the-supervisor, not the inside-burrow path. Mitigate by reusing the SeedsCliDeps shape from triggers, which is already exercised in production.

Race between operator-edits to `sd update --extensions` and warren's post-dispatch write: seeds uses shallow-merge so concurrent writes to different keys are safe; same-key writes are last-write-wins. Acceptable for denormalized pointer metadata.
seed_id added to runs but never written for legacy runs: nullable column + UI back-link gated on `seedId !== null` keeps backward compat. No backfill needed.

lastRunAt semantics drift (started-at vs finished-at): convention in ROADMAP.md:99 reads as start-time; reap-side rewrite would be a separate decision. Document the choice in the schema doc-comment so R-04 reads consistently.

updateExtensions failure leaving a stale seed: surface as a system event and a (future) `warren doctor` check that diffs runs.seed_id against seed.extensions.lastRunId. Filed as a follow-up seed if not in scope.

Trigger-string proliferation (manual / manual-trigger / cron / webhook / comment): zod enum locks it down; src/server/handlers.ts:354 currently uses `manual-trigger` and src/runs/spawn.ts:214 uses `manual`. Reconcile in step 2's schema.
acceptance: src/seeds-cli/ module exports updateExtensions + listScheduledSeeds + clearScheduledFor; src/triggers/seeds-extension.ts either deleted or a thin re-export. All existing trigger tests pass.
POST /runs accepts optional seedId; runs row persists seed_id; SQLite + Postgres migrations both land and acceptance scenario 19 still green.
After a manual POST /runs with seedId, `sd show <seed> --format json` shows extensions.{role,trigger:'manual',lastRunId,lastRunAt}.
After a cron fire, the seed's extensions show {scheduledFor:null, lastScheduledRun, lastRunId, lastRunAt, role, trigger:'cron'} — written via a single sd update.
Extension write failure emits a `seeds_extension_write_failed` system event on the run and does NOT abort the run (verified by test that injects a failing SpawnFn).
RunDetail UI shows a seed back-link when seedId is present.
New acceptance scenario `seeds-extensions-roundtrip` lives in scripts/acceptance/scenarios/ and is idempotent.
Quality gates pass: `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` clean, no warnings.
ROADMAP.md R-01 status updated; mulch convention recorded under `runs` domain; `sd sync && ml sync && git push` shows up-to-date with origin.

name: R-01 consumer-side: seeds extensions in spawn/UI

sections.context:
Today warren's agent registry has two tiers: built-ins shipped in `src/registry/builtins/` (claude-code, sapling, pi) and a single shared canopy library loaded from `CANOPY_REPO_URL`. There is no tier for roles that should travel with one project — e.g. a refactor-bot tuned to one codebase's conventions. Forcing project-specific roles into a shared library couples unrelated projects' role definitions and makes ad-hoc role iteration a five-step round-trip (clone canopy repo → write prompt → push → set env → refresh). R-03 closes the gap by scanning each project's `<projectPath>/.canopy/` as a third tier with precedence project > library > built-in, stamped on each agent row so the UI can show provenance. It is a load-bearing prerequisite for R-05 (in-UI roles editor) — without per-project storage there is nowhere safe for the editor to write — and rounds out R-04's roles picker, which surfaces a project tier with a `project:<id>` source label.

Key code shape today: `src/registry/canopy.ts` (the `cn` facade, cwd hardcoded to the library clone), `src/registry/refresh.ts` (drives `cn list` + `cn render` + `agents.upsert`), `src/registry/builtins/index.ts` (boot-time seeding), `src/db/repos/agents.ts` (keyed by `name` globally; `runs.agent_name` FK), `src/runs/spawn.ts:149` (`repos.agents.require(agentName)` — projectId-unaware), `src/server/handlers.ts` (`withAgentSource` decorates rows; `refreshAgents` is `POST /agents/refresh`). The agents table is a cache (`recoveryHint: "POST /agents/refresh to re-discover"`), which is what makes the schema change in step 1 viable.

Decisions locked before submission (2026-05-15): (1) `POST /agents/refresh` iterates all projects automatically after the library pass; (2) cross-tier inheritance (per-project `extends:` library/built-in) is deferred to a follow-up — V2 ships single-tier inheritance only; (3) on-disk render cache under `<projectPath>/.canopy/.rendered/` is deferred to a follow-up; (4) uniqueness is enforced at the DB layer via a composite unique on `(project_id, name)` plus a partial unique on `(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL`, with a synthetic rowid PK replacing today's `name` PK.
sections.approach:
Persist the per-project tier in the existing `agents` table by adding a nullable `project_id` column. Drop the single-column `name` primary key in favor of a synthetic rowid PK; enforce identity with two indexes: composite unique on `(project_id, name)` and partial unique on `(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL` (the latter guarantees one global row per name even though sqlite treats NULL as distinct). `NULL` means global tier (built-in or library); a non-null `project_id` means project-scoped. Matches the ROADMAP sketch (`POST /agents/refresh ... stamps the source onto each agents row (source: builtin | library | project:<projectId>)`).

The `runs.agent_name → agents.name` FK becomes incompatible with composite identity, so drop it. The agents table is already a soft cache that documents itself as 'refresh to re-discover'; spawn looks up `(name, projectId)` then falls back to `(name, NULL)` at the application layer.

For rendering, parameterize `CanopyClient`'s cwd so the same facade drives both the library clone and per-project `.canopy/` directories. Add `refreshProjectAgents(projectId)` next to `refreshAgentRegistry`. `POST /agents/refresh` does library + every project in one call (per-project errors collected, not fatal). `POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh` is the targeted path R-04/R-05 will call from the UI.

Source provenance: stamp `renderedJson.frontmatter.source = 'project:<projectId>'` for project-tier rows during refresh, the same way built-ins set `'builtin'` today. Widen `readAgentSource()` and the `AgentSource` type. Spawn-time freezing into `runs.rendered_agent_json` is unchanged.

Two ROADMAP open questions are deferred to follow-up seeds filed in step 10: cross-tier inheritance, and the on-disk rendered cache.

alternatives.name: Resolve per-project agents at spawn time only; don't persist
alternatives.rejected_because: Lighter, but loses the listing surface R-04's roles tab and R-05's editor need. Also makes `GET /agents` lie about what's available.

alternatives.name: Namespace the name (`<projectId>:<role>`) and keep agents PK as `name`
alternatives.rejected_because: Avoids the schema migration but pushes the project-scope concept into a string convention the UI has to parse — ugly and brittle, and the FK problem returns once you want clean listing.

alternatives.name: Composite PK + update runs.agent_name to a composite FK
alternatives.rejected_because: Strictly safer than dropping the FK, but rippling the change into `runs.agentName` (and every existing call site, plus both dialects) is far more invasive than the cache it would guard. The 'soft cache' framing of the agents table is the right precedent.

alternatives.name: Empty-string sentinel for global tier in a composite PK
alternatives.rejected_because: Removes the NULL-distinct problem but requires every read site to remember the sentinel, and the schema lies about identity (an empty string isn't 'no project'). Partial unique index is the right DB-layer expression.

steps.title: Schema migration: add agents.project_id (nullable), drop name PK, add composite + partial unique indexes, drop runs.agent_name FK
steps.description: In `src/db/schema/sqlite.ts` and `src/db/schema/postgres.ts`, on the `agents` table: replace `name TEXT PRIMARY KEY` with `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT` (sqlite) / `id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY` (postgres), add `project_id TEXT NULL`, and declare two indexes via drizzle's index builders — a composite unique on `(project_id, name)` and a partial unique on `(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL`. On `runs` in both dialects, remove the `.references(() => agents.name)` clause from `agent_name` (column stays `TEXT NOT NULL`, no FK). Write paired migrations: `src/db/migrations/0010_add_agents_project_id.sql` (sqlite — table rebuild via `ALTER TABLE agents RENAME TO __agents_old`, `CREATE TABLE agents (...)`, `INSERT INTO agents (name, rendered_json, registered_at, last_refreshed) SELECT ...`, `DROP TABLE __agents_old`, then `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX agents_project_name ON agents(project_id, name)` and `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX agents_name_global ON agents(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL`; the runs FK drop also requires a runs rebuild because sqlite cannot drop FKs in place) and `src/db/migrations/postgres/0003_add_agents_project_id.sql` (postgres — `ALTER TABLE agents DROP CONSTRAINT agents_pkey, ADD COLUMN id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ADD COLUMN project_id TEXT NULL`, `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX agents_project_name ON agents(project_id, name)`, `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX agents_name_global ON agents(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL`, `ALTER TABLE runs DROP CONSTRAINT runs_agent_name_agents_name_fk`). Update `src/db/schema/drift.test.ts` so the cross-dialect comparison continues to pass after the column add, the PK change, and the FK removal — adjust the FK assertion to expect zero FKs from `runs.agent_name` and assert the new indexes exist in both dialects. Existing rows are preserved as global-tier (`project_id` stays NULL).

steps.title: Widen AgentSource + source-stamping helpers
steps.description: In `src/registry/builtins/index.ts`, widen `AgentSource` to `'builtin' | 'library' | `project:${string}``. Update `readAgentSource()` to return the `project:<projectId>` literal when `frontmatter.source` starts with `project:`. Add a `stampProjectSource(definition, projectId)` helper that returns a new `AgentDefinition` with `frontmatter.source = `project:${projectId}``. Built-ins keep `frontmatter.source = 'builtin'`; library rows keep the absent-source → `'library'` fallback. Add unit tests in `src/registry/builtins/builtins.test.ts` for the project literal round-trip.

steps.title: Extend AgentsRepo with scope-aware reads + writes
steps.description: In `src/db/repos/agents.ts`, change every query to filter by `projectId` (undefined or null → `project_id IS NULL`; string → `project_id = ?`). New methods: `upsert({ name, projectId?, renderedJson })`, `getScoped({ name, projectId })`, `requireScoped({ name, projectId })`, `listAll({ projectId? })` where `undefined` returns all tiers, `null` returns global-only, and a string returns that project's tier only, `deleteScoped({ name, projectId })`. Add `requireForProject(name, projectId)` that tries `(name, projectId)` then `(name, NULL)` — this is the resolver `spawnRun` calls. Keep the existing zero-arg `listAll()` overload working by treating it as 'all tiers'. Cover same-name collision across two projects, global-tier fallback, and scoped delete leaving other tiers intact in `src/db/repos/agents.test.ts`.

steps.title: Parameterize CanopyClient cwd; add forProjectPath() factory
steps.description: In `src/registry/canopy.ts`, change `CanopyClient.invoke()` to accept an optional `cwd` per call (default stays `this.config.localDir`). Add `listAgents({ cwd? })` and `renderAgent(name, { cwd? })` signatures. Add a static `CanopyClient.forProjectPath(projectPath, baseConfig)` factory that returns a client whose default cwd is `<projectPath>/.canopy/`, sharing the same `cnBinary` + spawn + timeout settings. Update `canopy.test.ts` cwd assertions. Wire format and response shapes are unchanged.

steps.title: Add refreshProjectAgents() to scan + render a project's .canopy/
steps.description: New function in `src/registry/refresh.ts` mirroring `refreshAgentRegistry` scoped to one project: takes `{ projectId, projectPath, client, agents, prune?, now? }`. Probe `<projectPath>/.canopy/` with `existsSync`; if absent, return `{ registered: [], skipped: [], removed: [] }` (not an error). Otherwise, list via `cn list --tag agent --json` in the project cwd, render each, call `stampProjectSource(def, projectId)`, and `agents.upsert({ name, projectId, renderedJson })`. Per-agent errors collect into `skipped` (same posture as the library refresh). When `prune === true`, prune only rows where `project_id = projectId`. Add a unit test that uses an injected spawn to fake `cn`.

steps.title: HTTP surface: per-project refresh route + project filter on list/get
steps.description: In `src/server/handlers.ts`: add `refreshProjectAgentsHandler` for `POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh` — resolves the project, instantiates `CanopyClient.forProjectPath(project.localPath, ...)`, calls `refreshProjectAgents`, returns `{ projectId, registered, skipped, removed }`. Extend `refreshAgents` (the global handler) to also iterate every project after the library refresh, returning a response that includes the existing top-level `{ clone, registered, skipped, removed }` plus a new `projects: [{ projectId, registered, skipped, removed }]` array; per-project errors are collected, never fatal. Extend `listAgents` to accept `?projectId=<id>` — when set, return the global tier UNIONed with that project's tier; when absent, return the global tier only. Extend `getAgent` (`GET /agents/:name`) to accept `?projectId=` and use the same fallback resolution. Wire the new route in the handler array next to `/agents/refresh`. Update `src/server/types.ts` response shapes.

steps.title: Make spawn prefer project tier with fallback to global
steps.description: In `src/runs/spawn.ts` change `const agentRow = await input.repos.agents.require(input.agentName)` to `await input.repos.agents.requireForProject(input.agentName, input.projectId)`. No other spawn changes — `renderedAgentJson` already freezes whatever envelope it received, so a project-scoped agent's `frontmatter.source = 'project:<id>'` rides into the run row unchanged. Update `src/runs/spawn.test.ts` with a scenario where the same name resolves project-scoped for project A and global for project B.

steps.title: UI surface: client projectId filter + provenance badge on Agents/NewRun
steps.description: In `src/ui/src/api/client.ts`, widen `agents.list()` to accept `{ projectId? }` and propagate as a query param; widen the `AgentRow.source` type to include `project:${string}`. In `src/ui/src/pages/Agents.tsx`, render a small badge for project-tier rows showing the project name (look up via the existing projects list). In `src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx`, when a project is selected, call `agents.list({ projectId })` so project-scoped roles surface in the role picker; when no project is selected, behavior is unchanged. Add a 'Refresh project agents' affordance on the Agents page that POSTs `/projects/:id/agents/refresh`. This step delivers the minimum data + provenance surface R-04/R-05 will consume; it does not build R-04's full project-scoped nav or R-05's editor.

steps.title: Acceptance scenario: project tier roundtrip
steps.description: Add `scripts/acceptance/scenarios/22-canopy-project-tier.ts` (next slot after `21-claude-code-cost-smoke.ts`). Scenario: register a project whose repo has a committed `.canopy/<role>.md`, call `POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh`, assert `GET /agents?projectId=<id>` returns the project-tier row with `source: 'project:<id>'`, dispatch a run against that role name with the same project, wait for the run to reach a stable state, assert `runs.rendered_agent_json.frontmatter.source === 'project:<id>'`. Idempotent + self-cleaning per existing scenario conventions. Wire the new scenario id into the harness file that lists them.

steps.title: Docs + follow-up seeds
steps.description: Update `SPEC.md` §8.1 (agents) to document the three-tier precedence, the `project_id` column, the `source: 'project:<id>'` provenance, and the new `POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh` route. Mark R-03 `[shipped]` in `ROADMAP.md` with the plan id `pl-fef5`, and add an entry under `## Recently shipped`. Add a CHANGELOG entry under the next minor version. File two follow-up seeds: (a) 'Cross-tier inheritance: per-project canopy roles `extends:` library/built-in parents' (closing the ROADMAP R-03 open question; will require merging library prompts into the project's canopy index at refresh time); (b) 'On-disk rendered cache under `<projectPath>/.canopy/.rendered/` for `cn emit` compatibility' (pairs naturally with R-05's in-UI editor save path).

steps.title: Quality gates + mulch record + close
steps.description: Run `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck`. Run the new acceptance scenario against a live warren+burrow stack. Run `ml learn`, then `ml record warren --type decision --description 'agents table widened to (project_id, name) with rowid PK and partial unique on (name) WHERE project_id IS NULL; runs.agent_name FK dropped — agents are a soft cache, FK on a refreshable cache was load-bearing for nothing'` and `ml record warren --type pattern --description 'CanopyClient.forProjectPath() is the project-scoped factory; same facade drives library + project tier'`. Close child seeds, close warren-2842, `sd sync && ml sync && git push`.

risks: Dropping `runs.agent_name → agents.name` FK in sqlite requires a runs table rebuild (sqlite cannot drop FKs in place). The agents column-add + PK change also requires a rebuild. Both rebuilds in one migration, preserving rows; the `migrations.test.ts` parity check has to be green on both dialects before merging.

Sqlite treats NULL as distinct in unique indexes, so the composite `(project_id, name)` alone doesn't prevent two global rows for the same name. The partial unique `(name) WHERE project_id IS NULL` is the load-bearing index — confirm drizzle emits the `WHERE` clause for both sqlite and postgres, or write it raw in the migration.

Source-string parsing — anything that does `source === 'library'` instead of `source !== 'builtin'` breaks for project-tier rows. Grep callers of `readAgentSource` and the UI's source field; widen to `startsWith('project:')` where needed.

Per-project `cn list/render` runs in the project's worktree. A half-finished `.canopy/` write (missing index, malformed frontmatter) must skip gracefully — never let one project's bad prompt take down the global refresh. Mirror the library refresh's per-agent error collection.
Cross-tier inheritance is deferred but the ROADMAP says 'yes — that's the whole point.' The follow-up seed filed in step 10 must be visible to R-05 consumers so they don't assume V2 supports it.
acceptance: Migration applies cleanly on a sqlite DB with existing agent rows and on a postgres DB; `bun test` (including `migrations.test.ts`) is green on both dialects.
`agents` rows are addressable by `(name, projectId)` with `projectId` NULL for built-in/library and a concrete project id for project-tier rows. Partial unique index prevents duplicate global rows.
`POST /agents/refresh` refreshes the library AND every project's `.canopy/` in one call; per-project errors are collected and returned, never fatal.
`POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh` refreshes one project's tier and returns `{ projectId, registered, skipped, removed }`.
`GET /agents?projectId=<id>` returns global ∪ that project's tier; `GET /agents` (no filter) returns global only. `GET /agents/:name?projectId=<id>` resolves project-first with global fallback.

`POST /runs` with a name present in both tiers picks the project-tier row when `projectId` matches; falls back to global otherwise. `runs.rendered_agent_json.frontmatter.source` reflects the chosen tier.
Acceptance scenario `22-canopy-project-tier.ts` passes against a live warren+burrow stack.
UI shows a project-tier badge on the Agents page and surfaces project-scoped roles in NewRun's role picker when a project is selected.
`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` clean — Biome warnings count as failures per the repo gate.
ROADMAP R-03 marked `[shipped]` with plan id `pl-fef5`; SPEC §8.1 updated; CHANGELOG entry added; two follow-up seeds filed (cross-tier inheritance, on-disk rendered cache).

sections.context: R-19 (pl-2c59) shipped the per-run preview-environment infrastructure: schema, port allocator, reap-time launcher, host proxy + signed cookie, eviction worker, UI affordance, acceptance scenario, docs. Dogfooding the result against jayminwest.com (Next.js 14.2.24) on the warren-deployed (postgres) fly instance surfaced five distinct gaps that don't fit into any single child of pl-2c59 but together prevent a fresh-install operator from getting working previews for real-world SPA projects. Two are operator-visible bugs (vestigial sqlite-only teardown gate; unbounded probe fetch causing a 6m47s readiness wait against a 5m timeout); two are sidecar/launcher structural fixes (Next.js dev server binds to localhost-only by default, requiring a project-side `-H` workaround; install and dev-server bind share one timeout and one failure reason); one is the unlock that closes the loop (path-mode HTML loads but every root-relative asset hits warren's SPA fallback, blanking the preview). Without these, the R-19 ROADMAP claim that path mode is the zero-DNS default is technically true but operationally false for any modern SPA framework.
sections.approach: Adopt the five existing follow-up seeds into one feature plan and land them in ascending blast radius. Bug fixes ship first (warren-a743 is a one-line gate removal; warren-33eb adds an AbortController to one fetch call) so dogfooding stops being painful. Sidecar/launcher refactors land next (warren-79b2 hybridizes env + flag injection per CLI; warren-d9e7 splits `setup` from `command` in the schema, adding setup_failed/setup_timeout failure reasons). The SPA path-mode unlock (warren-63e1) lands last because it depends on the cleaner launcher foundation and is the largest behavior change (referer-routed asset proxying + per-run cookie name with Path=/ + abs-path HTML rewriter). Each step is an existing open seed adopted into this plan via `sd plan adopt --step <i>`; no new child seeds are spawned. Alternatives like batching the bugs into a separate plan or skipping the plan and using `sd dep add` were rejected because the dogfood-driven arc reads as a single coherent unit and one plan revision is easier for reviewers to trace.

steps.title: Drop sqlite-only gate in /runs/:id/preview/teardown handler (warren-a743)
Add per-call AbortController timeout to preview readiness probe fetch (warren-33eb)
Inject HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 (+HOST/PORT) and emit Next.js -H flag in sidecar env (warren-79b2)
Split `setup` from `command` in preview schema for separate timeout + eager-boot prep (warren-d9e7)
Path-mode out-of-the-box for SPAs: referer routing + per-run cookie + abs-path HTML rewrite (warren-63e1)

acceptance: All five child seeds (warren-a743, warren-33eb, warren-79b2, warren-d9e7, warren-63e1) are adopted into this plan at step indices 1–5 respectively.
Each child closes with its own per-seed acceptance criteria intact (see individual seed descriptions).
Operator on the warren-deployed (postgres) instance can click Teardown on a live preview and get a 200, not a 503 (warren-a743).
Preview launcher's readiness loop can never block past `readiness_timeout` due to a single hung probe call (warren-33eb).
A Next.js 14.x project under `.warren/preview.yaml` boots and serves traffic without any project-side `-H 0.0.0.0` workaround (warren-79b2).
Operators can declare `preview.setup` separately from `preview.command`; install failures surface as `preview_failure_message: setup failed: …` distinct from readiness failures (warren-d9e7).

A fresh-install operator pointing warren at a stock Next.js / Vite / CRA project gets a working preview at `/p/<id>/` with all root-relative assets resolving correctly, no per-project HTML changes (warren-63e1).
`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` pass after each child lands.

name: Preview reliability & SPA support hardening (post-jayminwest.com dogfood)
